title: Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
text:
Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) Scheme
Share link
Supplements eligible workers' income and CPF savings through cash and CPF payments.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Eligible employees, platform workers and self-employed persons can receive cash and CPF payments to supplement their income and grow their CPF savings for retirement, housing and healthcare
Employees can receive up to $4,900 per year
Self-employed persons and Platform Workers can receive up to $3,267 per year
Collapse all sections
About this support

Employees

From Work Year 2025, eligible recipients will receive monthly payments of up to $4,900 per year, comprising 40% in cash and 60% credited to CPF Accounts




Self-employed persons and Platform Workers

From Work Year 2025, eligible recipients will receive annual payments of up to $3,267, comprising 10% cash and 90% credited to MediSave Account

Self-employed persons will receive payments annually, while Platform Workers will receive payments monthly

From Work Year 2029, eligible Platform Workers whose CPF contribution rates fully align with that of employees will receive higher WIS payments at the same level as employees

Who is this for?

Eligibility criteria

30 years old and above as at 31 December of the Work Year

Singapore Citizen

Employee, self-employed person and/or platform worker

Gross monthly income of $500* to $3,000 (if married, assessable income of recipient's spouse must not exceed $70,000), for the month worked

Average gross monthly income of not more than S$3,000 in the past 12 months as an employee or platform worker

Annual value of the place of residence (as indicated on NRIC) must not exceed $21,000, as at 31 December of the preceding year

Does not own more than 1 property (if married, recipient and spouse must not own more than 1 property)

*Persons with disabilities, workers who qualify for ComCare Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance and caregivers of care recipients (generally include caregivers residing with care recipients who are medically certified to have permanent moderate to severe disabilities) will qualify for concessionary WIS, even if they earn less than $500.

How to apply?

 Employees

No application required

Your WIS eligibility will be automatically assessed based on the income declared by your employer(s) for work done in that month

Self-employed persons

Declare your net trade income to IRAS and make the required MediSave contributions in full

Platform workers

No application required

Your WIS eligibility will be automatically assessed based on the net earnings declared by your platform operator(s) for work done in that month

Where can I find help?

View more details

CPFB Hotline: 1800 227 1188

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Note: By appointment only

Scheme last updated 27 Apr 2026
